mapping.a_element: linearized Boltzmann equation for the 2D electron distribution function
mapping.b_element: Boltzmann equation for plasma-particle distributions (Liouville operator + collision integrals)

mapping.a_element: electron-electron collision integral that relaxes perturbations of the distribution (angular harmonics)
mapping.b_element: collision integrals / interaction rates that relax out-of-equilibrium perturbations

mapping.a_element: streaming/transport term driven by the magnetic field B (method of characteristics)
mapping.b_element: Liouville operator with the moving bubble wall sourcing out-of-equilibrium perturbations

mapping.a_element: non-equilibrium perturbation delta-f of the electron distribution
mapping.b_element: out-of-equilibrium perturbations of the plasma-particle distributions

mapping.a_element: transport observable extracted: magnetoresistance R(B) / kinematic viscosity
mapping.b_element: transport observable extracted: bubble-wall friction / wall velocity

brief_justification: Both compute a transport/dissipation quantity by solving the same governing equation - the linearized Boltzmann kinetic equation with a Liouville streaming operator and a collision integral - for out-of-equilibrium perturbations of a particle distribution function; paper A applies it to 2D electron flow (Corbino magnetoresistance/viscosity) and paper B to plasma particles at a bubble wall (friction/wall velocity), with the electron-electron collision integral playing the role of the plasma interaction rates and the magnetic-field-driven streaming playing the role of the wall-sourced Liouville term.
